data.title: Wall Street Analysts Back Intel's (INTC) New CEO Lip-Bu Tan on Turnaround Hopes
data.description:
Intel (INTC) just made a major move by naming Lip-Bu Tan as its new permanent CEO, pushing its shares up more than 10%. He will officially take over on March 18, 2025, replacing interim co-CEOs David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus. Analysts are buzzing about the announcement, with many on Wall Street believing that Tan's deep industry knowledge and leadership could drive a much-needed turnaround for the struggling chipmaker.

Intel's New CEO Lip-Bu Tan Outlines His Vision

Lip-Bu Tan admitted that Intel has faced tough times but said he believes the company can bounce back. In a message to employees, he said, "We have a truly unique opportunity to remake our company at one of the most pivotal moments in its history." He stressed that Intel will be an “engineering-focused company” under his leadership. At the same time, he made it clear that delivering for customers will remain Intel's top priority.

Tan ended his message by calling this phase "The New Intel," where the company will learn from past mistakes and work toward becoming both a strong products company and a competitive foundry.

Analysts Weigh in on Intel's New CEO

Wall Street is betting that Tan's leadership could help Intel regain its place in the tough chip market. Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers maintained an Equal Weight rating on Intel with a $25 price target after the company announced Tan's appointment. He sees the hire as a positive step, believing it could speed up or reshape Intel's Foundry strategy. While Tan's appointment is significant on its own, Rakers says investors are more focused on his openness to bold moves, like a potential spin-off or other major changes.

Meanwhile, Stifel analysts also see Tan's appointment as a positive for Intel's long-term outlook. They pointed to his success in transforming Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) and his investment expertise in the semiconductor industry. However, they cautioned that Intel's turnaround will take time. They noted that the company lags behind in AI infrastructure and argued that firms like NVIDIA (NVDA) are better positioned for near-term gains.

Stifel added that a clearer view of Tan's vision in the coming months could be a key driver for Intel's stock.

Intel Faces Big Challenges Ahead

Despite the excitement, Tan will face tough challenges in transforming Intel's future. The company has fallen behind rivals in chip technology and has struggled with delays in its foundry business. The company also reported a massive $18.8 billion loss in 2024.

It would be interesting to see if the new leadership can bring the chip giant back to the top.

Is INTC Stock a Buy?

The stock of Intel has a consensus Hold rating among 31 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on one Buy, 25 Hold, and five Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average INTC price target of $22.76 implies 10.06% upside from current levels.

See more INTC analyst ratings

data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Intel (INTC) Stock Jumps 10% as Company Announces New CEO
data.description:
Intel (INTC) stock is up 10% in after hours trading on news that the chipmaker has named Lip-Bu Tan as its new permanent CEO.

Tan previously served as CEO of Cadence Design Systems (CDNS), which makes software used by all the major chip designers, including Intel. Tan has also served on the board of directors at Intel, although he departed last year saying he had other commitments.

Tan replaces Intel’s interim co-CEOs David Zinsner and MJ Holthaus, who took over running the company last December after the abrupt departure of former CEO Pat Gelsinger. Tan is also rejoining Intel's board. He takes the helm of the chipmaker as it tries to recover from a tumultuous four-year run under Pat Gelsinger when its stock declined nearly 75%.

Way Forward

Under Gelsinger’s leadership, Intel spent billions of dollars in an effort to pivot from solely designing microchips and processors to also producing them through a new foundry business. The costly and difficult transition ultimately cost Gelsinger his job as investors pressured the company to reduce costs and spinoff businesses amid declining sales.

Intel is in the process of building a $20 billion chip factory in Ohio. The company has been accused of losing focus and missing the booming artificial intelligence (AI) market and losing ground to rivals such as Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD). INTC stock was removed from the Dow Jones Industrial Average last year and replaced by Nvidia.

Tan now becomes the fourth permanent CEO at Intel in the last seven years. There are rumors that Intel might be a takeover target, although no deals have yet emerged. INTC stock has declined 52% in the last 12 months.

Is INTC Stock a Buy?

The stock of Intel has a consensus Hold rating among 31 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on one Buy, 25 Hold, and five Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average INTC price target of $22.76 implies 10.06% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on INTC stock

data.title: Hedge Funds Are Dumping Stocks at Fastest Pace in Four Years, Says Goldman Sachs (GS)
data.description:
Hedge funds are dumping their stock holdings at the fastest pace in four years as tariffs and signs of an economic slowdown in the U.S. continue to roil markets.

Data from Goldman Sachs' (GS) prime brokerage unit shows that professional money managers and hedge fund traders are selling stocks and covering their short bets at the fastest rate since 2021 when inflation began to rise dramatically and the prospect of higher interest rates came into view.

Hedge funds are retreating from equities as the macroeconomic environment in the U.S. has grown less clear, and as U.S. President Donald Trump's aggressive tariffs on foreign imports into America creates volatility in equity markets.

Recession Fears

Goldman Sachs has blamed the decline in the stock market in recent weeks, with the benchmark S&P 500 index down 9% from its recent peak, partly on rapid selling among hedge funds and other money managers. Earlier in the day, Goldman Sachs' lowered its year-end target on the S&P 500 index to 6,200 from 6,500, the first major Wall Street firm to do so.

According to Goldman, hedge funds have been selling technology and industrial stocks the most in recent days. Speaking to CNBC, Brad Gerstner, CEO of the Altimeter Capital hedge fund, said he’s lowered his stock holdings to the bottom decile of the firm's risk exposure “We have high economic uncertainty, high political uncertainty, and high technological uncertainty,” he said in explaining his decision.

Is GS Stock a Buy?

The stock of Goldman Sachs has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 15 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 11 Buy and four Hold recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average GS price target of $675.00 implies 25.28% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on GS stock

data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: PATH Earnings: UiPath Tanks 16% on Soft Guidance
data.description:
Shares of software provider UiPath (PATH) tanked in after-hours trading after the company reported earnings for its fourth quarter of Fiscal Year 2025. Earnings per share came in at $0.26, which beat analysts’ consensus estimate of $0.20 per share. In addition, sales increased by 4.6% year-over-year, with revenue hitting $424 million. However, this missed analysts’ expectations of $425.11 million.

UiPath’s CEO, Daniel Dines, said that he is proud of the company’s progress in the past year. Indeed, he said that the firm introduced many new artificial intelligence features and innovative products like Autopilot, Agent Builder, and Agentic Orchestration. And although investors are disappointed (based on their reactions to the results), Dines is happy with the company’s stability and growth.

Separately, UiPath also announced that it has acquired Peak AI Limited, which is a company that specializes in using AI to help businesses make better decisions. Peak’s technology focuses on industries like retail and manufacturing, where it can help optimize inventory and pricing. This means businesses can get results quickly without needing a large team of tech experts.

2026 Outlook

Looking forward, management has provided the following guidance for 2026:

  • Q1 Revenue of between $330 million and $335 million versus analysts’ estimates of $367.3 million
  • Q1 Non-GAAP operating profit of $45 million versus expectations of $46.9 million
  • FY Revenue of between $1.525 billion and $1.530 billion versus analysts’ estimates of $1.585 billion
  • FY Non-GAAP operating profit of $270 million versus expectations of $239.3 million

As we can see, the company’s outlook mostly missed expectations. This, along with the mixed Q4 results, is what led to the stock plunging in after-hours trading.

Is PATH Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on PATH stock based on four Holds assigned in the past three months. In addition, the average PATH price target of $15.33 per share implies 28.5% upside potential. However, it’s worth noting that estimates will likely change following today’s earnings report.

data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Stock Is Trading at a "Compelling Price," Says Bank of America
data.description:
The stock of mega-cap chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) is trading at a “very compelling price” right now, according to Bank of America (BAC).

Vivek Arya, a top five-star rated analyst, is urging investors to consider buying NVDA stock ahead of the company’s upcoming GTC AI Developers Conference that’s being held March 17-21, which could prove to be a catalyst for the company and its shares.

In a note to clients, Arya reiterated a Buy rating on NVDA stock with a $200 price target, which is 74% higher than where the shares currently trade. The analyst said the stock looks very attractive trading at its current price and valuation, having fallen 14% so far in 2025.

Important Updates

Arya said that he anticipates a rally in NVDA stock coming out of the March developers’ conference because it is expected to include important updates on Nvidia’s Blackwell Ultra microchip and Rubin, the company’s next-generation networking processor.

Nvidia is also supposed to provide updates on its future plans in area such as autonomous vehicles, robotics, and quantum computing. In his note, Arya stressed that NVDA stock is trading at a lower price-to-earnings ratio than its expected earnings growth rate, which indicates it is undervalued relative to the company’s future earnings potential.

NVDA stock has risen 27% over the last 12 months.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

The stock of Nvidia has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 42 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 39 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average NVDA price target of $177.41 implies 53.60% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on NVDA stock

data.title: "Quantum Computational Supremacy" Sends D-Wave Quantum Stock (QBTS) Surging
data.description:
D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) has achieved “quantum computational supremacy” with its annealing quantum computer. This means that D-Wave’s computer has solved complex problems more efficiently than classical supercomputers. Interestingly, the firm’s achievement was published in a peer-reviewed paper in the journal Science. As a result, shares of D-Wave surged on Wednesday, along with those of other quantum computing stocks, such as Quantum Computing (QUBT), IonQ (IONQ), and Rigetti Computing (RGTI).

This breakthrough is a big deal because it shows that quantum computers can be used for practical applications. The problems that D-Wave’s computer solved were related to simulating the behavior of complex materials. This kind of simulation is important for developing new materials and technologies. Unsurprisingly, D-Wave’s CEO Alan Baratz said that the achievement was an industry first and noted that previous claims of quantum supremacy had been disputed or lacked practical value.

It is worth noting that the implications of this breakthrough are significant. Indeed, quantum computing has the potential to completely revolutionize many industries, from medicine to finance. Companies like Google (GOOGL), which recently revealed its own quantum computing chip called Willow, are already investing in quantum computing research. While we’re still in the early days of quantum computing, breakthroughs like this one show that the technology is advancing at a fast pace.

Which Quantum Computing Stock Is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the four stocks mentioned above, analysts think that IONQ stock has the most room to run. In fact, IONQ's average price target of $41.60 per share implies 90% upside potential. On the other hand, analysts expect the least from QUBT stock since they are not even covering it.

data.title: Robinhood (HOOD) Sees Crypto Trading Volumes Plunge 29% amid Market Rout
data.description:
Robinhood Markets (HOOD) has reported a 29% plunge in crypto trading volumes on its platform during February as retail investors lose interest in digital coins and tokens.

Robinhood added that the decrease in crypto trading far outpaced declines seen in equities and options trading, which dipped only 1% in February. The drop off in crypto trading volumes comes amid a slump in the broader market, with Bitcoin's (BTC) price falling nearly 25% since U.S. President Donald Trump's inauguration on Jan. 20.

The steep decline in crypto trading volumes at Robinhood is not good news for other crypto exchanges such as Coinbase Global (COIN) that depend on active trading among investors. Crypto trading on Robinhood's platform in February totaled $14.40 billion, down nearly 30% from a year earlier, according to the company.

Waning Enthusiasm

Robinhood says that trading volumes have continued to decline as the crypto market slides lower, with Bitcoin's price dropping 15% in the last month to currently trade at $82,700. Most crypto exchanges, including Robinhood and Coinbase, cater to individual retail investors and a lack of interest on their part could hurt the companies’ financial results.

The slump in crypto trading is a sharp reversal for Robinhood, which reported that its revenue from trading cryptocurrencies rose more than 700% in fourth-quarter 2024 as mania gripped the markets following U.S. President Donald Trump’s re-election last November. HOOD stock is up 5% this year.

Is HOOD Stock a Buy?

The stock of Robinhood Markets has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 18 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 13 Buy and five Hold recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average HOOD price target of $71 implies 80.48% upside from current levels.

Questions or Comments about the article? Write to editor@tipranks.com
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: GOOGL, DPZ, PEP: Goldman Sachs Names Best Stocks to Buy Now
data.description:
U.S. investment bank Goldman Sachs (GS) has published a list of stocks that it feels can help investors weather the current market storm.

The Wall Street firm published the list shortly after it lowered its year-end target for the S&P 500 to 6,200 from 6,500. The downgrade comes as the S&P 500 has fallen 9% from its all-time high in the last three weeks and is near a correction, defined as a pullback of 10% or more from recent highs.

However, even though Goldman has turned more bearish on the stock market overall, it still sees opportunities when it comes to individual stocks. The investment bank’s Chief U.S. Equity Strategist David Kostin has named several stocks that it feels can provide investors with shelter from the market storm. 

Top Picks

Kostin's team says they have identified stable stocks for clients to buy to ride out a possible recession and bear market in the U.S. The firm has selected stocks that have increased cash flow reliably over the past decade. The stocks are also projected by Goldman analysts to have stable or higher earnings this year.

The stocks list in the “stable growth basket” include Alphabet (GOOGL), one of the only tech names on the list, as well as the Domino's Pizza (DPZ) restaurant chain, and soft drink and packaged goods company PepsiCo (PEP). Each of these stocks has a decent valuation right, now is likely to grow earnings this year, and is likely to see minimal disruption due to tariffs or in a recession, says Goldman.

Other stocks to make the list include Bank of New York Mellon (BK), Gilead Sciences (GILD), and Waste Management (WM).

Is GS Stock a Buy?

The stock of Goldman Sachs has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 15 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 11 Buy and four Hold recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average GS price target of $675.00 implies 25.28% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on GS stock

data.title: Binance Secures $2 Billion as Abu Dhabi-based MGX Joins as First Institutional Investor
data.description:
Binance just landed a massive $2 billion investment from Abu Dhabi-based MGX, marking a major milestone for the world's largest crypto exchange. CoinDesk reports that this is the first institutional placement in Binance and MGX's first-ever investment in digital assets. The deal was made in stablecoins, though the exact currency was not disclosed.

Binance Expands Its Presence in the UAE

This investment strengthens Binance's position in the United Arab Emirates. The exchange already has around 1,000 employees in Abu Dhabi and operates under a Virtual Asset Service Provider (VASP) license in Dubai. CoinDesk notes that Binance's Abu Dhabi subsidiary also holds a regulatory license to provide custody services.

Binance CEO Has Deep Ties to Abu Dhabi

Binance CEO Richard Teng has strong connections in the region. He previously led the Abu Dhabi Financial Services Regulatory Authority before taking the top job at Binance.

Now that regulatory approvals and MGX’s fresh capital are boosting confidence in Binance, it will be interesting to see how the exchange strengthens its foothold in the Middle East's crypto market. This investment comes as crypto prices remain volatile. Investors should track crypto prices on the TipRanks Cryptocurrency Center, especially as Binance's expansion could drive renewed interest in digital assets.

Questions or Comments about the article? Write to editor@tipranks.com
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Tesla Investors Panic as Survey Shows Musk's Politics Hurting Stock
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) investors are worried. A Morgan Stanley (MS) survey found that 85% of respondents believe Elon Musk's political involvement is hurting the company. CNBC reports that 45% called the effect “negative,” while another 40% said it was “extremely negative.” The timing couldn't be worse. Tesla shares have already plunged nearly 40% this year.

Tesla Deliveries Expected to Decline in 2025

It's not just politics that have investors spooked. The survey also revealed that 59% of respondents expect Tesla to deliver fewer cars this year. Worse, 21% predicted deliveries would drop by more than 10%. Reports of vandalism against Tesla vehicles have only added to concerns.

Musk Admits Struggles Running Tesla while in Government

Musk's growing political profile isn't helping. His role in President Donald Trump's administration has made him a target. CNBC reports that Musk admitted he is now running Tesla “with great difficulty.” Investors aren't convinced he can juggle both worlds.

On Monday, Tesla shares crashed 15%, their worst single-day loss since 2020.

Is Tesla a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 12 Holds, and 12 Sells assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $332.97 per share implies 36.6% upside potential.

See more TSLA analyst ratings
